chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No.D-2264 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). D.O matter 1. For order on CMA No.16959/2025 (U/A). 2. For order on CMA No.12703/2025 (Exp). 3. For order on CMA No.12704/2025 (Surety)..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 26.08.2025 Mr. Asfandyar Jahangir, Advocate for Applicant/Petitioner. Mr. Ali Hyder Saleem, Addl. P.G..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption is allowed subject to all just exceptions. 3. This is an application for return of surety amount which was deposited by the applicant/surety in compliance of the order dated 26.05.2025, whereby, the petitioner/accused was granted interim pre-arrest bail subject to furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Nazir endorsement dated 26.05.2025 confirms that the present applicant/surety stood surety by depositing an amount of Rs.100,000-/. Learned counsel for the applicant/surety submits that on 11.06.2025, the I.O. made a statement before this Court that he had filed the challan before the concerned Court and the petitioner is no more required in the investigation and accordingly the instant petition was dismissed having become infructuous on the basis of such statement of the I.O. He, therefore, prays that surety amount is no more required by this Court and the same may be returned to the applicant/surety. Learned Addl. P.G. has recorded no objection. In view of the above, the surety furnished by the applicant is no longer required to be retained by this Court. Accordingly, the application for return of surety is allowed.

In view of the above, the surety furnished by the applicant is no longer required to be retained by this Court. Accordingly, the application for return of surety is allowed. Office is directed to return the surety amount to the applicant/surety as per rules after proper verification, identification and after obtaining a valid receipt on record. JUDGE JUDGE Jamil
